\title{OpenLSM | Actuation Comparison}
\author{William Bowley}
\date{\today}

\begin{document}
\maketitle

\section{Overview} 
This document explores whether replacing stepper-driven gantry actuation in Fused Deposition Modeling (FDM) systems with
Tubular Linear Synchronous Motors (TLSM) meaningfully shifts system-level constraints.

\vspace{0.5cm}
The analysis focuses on whether extrusion mass flow rate imposes a hard upper bound on performance, independent of 
improvement in motion dynamics. If this constraint dominates, increased actuator bandwidth and acceleration will not translate into
higher print speed, but may reduce motion-induced defects such as `belt-pattern', ghosting and ringing.

\vspace{0.5cm}
The objective is not to produce a final design, but to determine whether TLSM actuation enables a different operating regime and
to identify the parameters that governs its effectiveness in FDM systems

\end{document}
